# U Local AI

> A testbed for Chrome's built-in Prompt API (`window.LanguageModel`), which runs Gemini Nano
> on-device. It is a ChatGPT-style chat where the model is local, conversations live in
> IndexedDB, and no network request is ever made — no backend, no accounts, no analytics.

The whole app is a single static page served by a ~50-line dependency-free Node server. It
requires Chrome with `chrome://flags/#prompt-api-for-gemini-nano` enabled; without it the app
shows a screen explaining what is missing.

## What it exercises

- Model lifecycle — `availability()`, `params()` and the download monitor with live progress.
- Sessions — `create()` with a system prompt, `temperature` and `topK`, replayed history, `destroy()`.
- Generation — `promptStreaming()` token by token, with an abort button.
- Limits — context usage against `inputQuota` and the `quotaoverflow` event.

It is also a PWA with an offline service worker, light/dark themes, pt/en i18n, and an event
log that shows what the API is doing.

## Pages

- [Home](https://ulocalai.com/): the app itself — the only indexable page.
- [Source](https://github.com/VCTGomes/ulocalai): the project is MIT-licensed and open source.

## Optional

- Conversation routes (`/chat/<id>`, `/new`) render the same shell and hold no public
  content; conversations are private to the browser that created them.
- Answers come from a small on-device model and can be wrong or incomplete. There is no
  fallback to a hosted model.
